behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Catching the real failure (embedding model API changes its output dimension, incompatible with the index) requires calling the actual embedding service API. A mocked embedding model could return any dimension without revealing dimension mismatches or API incompatibilities. The integration between embedding output and vector database schema can only be verified by calling the real embedding API.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding API with a sample query string and verify the returned vector has exactly N dimensions (where N matches the vector database index schema). Optionally, attempt to insert the vector into the actual vector database to confirm structural compatibility. Repeat with multiple query formats to ensure consistency.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test correctly targets System level: requires real external embedding API call.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: A mock vector database could always return results in the correct order, hiding real bugs in the database's ordering or our code's handling of the results. The real failure is that the vector database returns results in wrong order or our sorting logic is broken. This requires querying an actual (or test) vector database instance with known vectors and similarity scores.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a test vector database with a set of vectors with known similarity scores to a query vector. Execute a similarity search and verify returned results are ordered by descending cosine similarity. Include edge cases: multiple vectors with identical scores, and a single distant outlier. Verify order consistency across repeated queries.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test correctly targets Integration level: requires real vector database instance (local or containerized), not mocked.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: A mocked LLM could be hardcoded to return a different order, hiding the real failure: the production LLM ignores query nuance or returns the same ranking as similarity search. LLM behavior is non-deterministic—running the same query 100 times may yield different re-rankings. Catching real re-ranking failures requires calling the actual LLM with realistic intent-heavy queries and observing whether it produces meaningfully different orderings based on query semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Feed the real LLM re-ranker with top-K search results and a query containing nuanced intent (e.g., 'coffee near transit' vs. 'coffee near airport'). Call the real LLM multiple times (at least 5 runs) with the same inputs and verify it produces a different ranking from the raw similarity ranking at least once. Validate by examining the LLM's reasoning output to confirm re-ranking is based on query intent, not random noise.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test correctly targets Agentic level: depends on real LLM's non-deterministic output to verify behavior.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: A mock database could always return empty results, hiding threshold-filtering bugs. The real failure is that the system returns low-similarity results instead of filtering them out. Testing requires a real (test) vector database populated with known vectors, queried with unrelated terms, and verifying the similarity threshold correctly filters out low-match results.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a test vector database with domain-specific vectors (e.g., electronics products). Query with an unrelated string (e.g., 'pasta recipes'). Verify that with a similarity threshold (e.g., 0.5), the result set is empty. Test boundary conditions: set threshold to 0.99 and verify results remain empty; lower threshold below the maximum similarity score and verify results reappear. Confirm threshold filtering works consistently across multiple unrelated queries.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test correctly targets Integration level: requires real vector database with threshold logic, not mocked.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: Latency bottlenecks only appear when all real components (embedding API, vector database, LLM re-ranker) operate together. Mocked embeddings and LLMs would not reveal network delays, database query time, or LLM inference latency. The real failure is the production pipeline exceeds 2 seconds. Testing requires orchestrating multiple agentic/infrastructure steps in sequence, measuring true end-to-end latency across realistic queries.
behaviors.test_description: Send natural language queries (e.g., 'durable water bottles under $30') through the entire pipeline: embedding generation → vector database similarity search → LLM re-ranking. Measure elapsed time from API request receipt to response delivery. Run at least 10 iterations with diverse queries to account for LLM non-determinism and variable latency. Verify median latency is below 2 seconds and document the 95th percentile.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test correctly targets Workflow level: requires full multi-step pipeline with real embedding, vector DB, and LLM components.
